response.justification: Bogdan has a genuine geospatial background through GISCollective and Green Map, and solid distributed-systems exposure from his Dunnhumby tenure, but the role's core requirement — production-grade C++ or Rust — is not credibly met. His C++ usage is isolated to a 2014–2015 stint at National Instruments and a student thesis project; his primary commercial languages are JavaScript/TypeScript and DLang. There is no routing-tile, navigation pipeline, or map-data transformation experience anywhere in the resume, and no Rust is listed at all. The GIS work is web-layer (Ember.js/Node.js map display) rather than the low-level tile generation and pipeline optimization the role demands.
strengths: Genuine geospatial domain exposure via GISCollective (event-driven geo-data platform, FOSS4G talk) and Green Map GIS application
Demonstrated ability to build low-latency, high-throughput distributed services (millions of HTTP requests/second at Dunnhumby using async I/O and high-performance databases)
Strong quality-engineering culture: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and a track record of teaching testing workshops
concerns: C++ experience is narrow and dated (NI LabWindows 2014–2015 and a bachelor-thesis audio project); no Rust experience at all — the two primary technology requirements are effectively unmet

No routing, navigation guidance, or tile-pipeline work anywhere in a 17-year career; geospatial contributions are web-GIS visualization, not the data-transformation and latency-optimization layer this role operates in
Current and recent trajectory (TypeScript/Node.js, AI-agent tooling, direct-mail pricing) moves further from systems programming and geospatial pipelines, not toward them
